title: Encapsulate cross-cutting field behavior with property wrappers
description: Use property wrappers to factor out repeated get/set behavior (clamping, persistence, thread-safety) instead of duplicating logic per property.
body: Use when several properties share the same access behavior (validation, UserDefaults backing, clamping, logging). Do not hand-roll the same didSet/computed logic on every property or hide IO in a wrapper used in a hot path unknowingly. Actions: define a @propertyWrapper struct with wrappedValue (and projectedValue via $ when callers need the wrapper), keep its work cheap and predictable, and document any side effects (a UserDefaults-backed wrapper does IO on every set). Validation signals include unit tests on the wrapper's get/set behavior and instruments showing no surprise IO. Risk boundary: a wrapper that performs IO or locking on every access can silently dominate cost; verify with Instruments and escalate before placing an IO-backed wrapper on a frequently-mutated property.
